title: mentha arvensis var. villosa
intro: Mentha arvensis var. villosa, also known as woolly wild mint, is a variety of the wild mint plant, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of woolly wild mint include relief from digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to aid in weight loss and improve mental clarity. The herb can be consumed as a tea, infused in oils for topical use, or added to skincare products, making it a versatile and valuable addition to herbal preparations.
